summary: Matched the ftCommon FlyReflect translation unit by changing ftCo_FlyReflect.c source shaping, renaming several .sdata2 symbols back to anonymous compiler-style labels, and flipping configure.py from NonMatching to Matching for melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_FlyReflect.c. The decomp.dev report credited 4 new matches: .sdata2 plus ftCo_800C17CC, ftCo_800C18A8, and ftCo_FlyReflect_Coll, with +1268 matched code bytes and +32 matched data bytes.

key_files.role: Main matching work for FlyReflect collision/reflection helpers and physics/collision callbacks.
Changed five ftCo_804D8C40..58 .sdata2 symbols to anonymous @221/@259/@260/@261/@262 labels to match data layout/naming.
Promoted ftCo_FlyReflect.c from Object(NonMatching, ...) to Object(Matching, ...).

what_changed: Replaced broad includes in ftCo_FlyReflect.c with a narrower set including placeholder.h and ftCommon/inlines.h, while removing platform.h, math.h, math_ppc.h, and several direct fighter/ftCommon headers from this file.
Adjusted stack padding in ftCo_800C15F4 and ftCo_800C1718 from byte arrays to explicit u32 pad locals.
Wrapped ftCo_800C1718 in #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on / #pragma pop and changed its Fighter access to gobj->user_data.
Added ftCo_800C1718_inline, duplicating the ceiling-reflect check/body for call sites that needed inline codegen.
Expanded ftCo_800C17CC from two helper calls into explicit wall/ceiling reflect control flow, including PAD_STACK(0x10), local temporaries, and explicit ret handling.
Added fake_sqrtf using __frsqrte plus Newton-Raphson refinement and a volatile float result, then used it in ftCo_800C18A8 instead of sqrtf for velocity magnitude.
Reshaped ftCo_800C18A8 local declaration/order and Fighter pointer assignment.
Expanded ftCo_FlyReflect_Coll return logic by using ftCo_800C1718_inline at one callsite and explicit tmp-based branching for ftCo_800C15F4/ftCo_800C1718 at another.
Updated configure.py so ftCo_FlyReflect.c is built as Matching.
Updated GALE01 symbols so newly matched .sdata2 constants use anonymous @ labels instead of ftCo_804D8Cxx names.
smart_moves: Used local-variable and control-flow reshaping rather than semantic changes: the diff preserves FlyReflect wall/ceiling reflection behavior while altering codegen-sensitive details.
Separated a non-inlined ftCo_800C1718 from an explicit inline duplicate, allowing different call sites to match their expected call-vs-inline assembly patterns.
Replaced sqrtf with a local fake_sqrtf based on __frsqrte and repeated refinement, likely matching the original compiler/runtime sequence in ftCo_800C18A8.
Used PAD_STACK(0x10), u32 pad locals, volatile storage, and explicit ret/tmp integers to influence stack frame and branch/register allocation.
Cleaned symbol names for .sdata2 constants to anonymous @ labels when evidence suggested they were compiler-generated constants rather than meaningful named globals.
decomp_lessons: For small last-mile mismatches, preserve behavior but try matching the original compiler's exact decisions: inline-vs-call boundaries, local declaration order, explicit boolean temporaries, and stack padding can each matter.
If a stack pad expressed as an initialized byte array creates unwanted rodata, an explicit PAD_STACK macro or scalar zeroed pad locals may avoid introducing extra data while still shaping the frame.

The same helper logic may need both non-inlined and inlined forms in one file; use #pragma dont_inline for the callable version and a separate inline duplicate when call sites demand different codegen.

Math library calls are not always equivalent for matching. A hand-shaped reciprocal-square-root approximation with __frsqrte, double constants, and volatile float storage can be necessary to reproduce original PowerPC codegen.
Anonymous .sdata2 constants should remain anonymous @NNN labels unless there is strong evidence for a meaningful symbol name.
Promote the object to Matching in configure.py only after source and data sections are fully matched; this PR's bot report confirmed 100% for the affected .sdata2 and functions.
naming_conventions: File and function naming follows ftCo_ for common fighter code, e.g. ftCo_FlyReflect.c, ftCo_800C15F4, ftCo_800C1718, ftCo_800C17CC, ftCo_800C18A8.
Motion state names retain ftCo_MS_ prefixes, e.g. ftCo_MS_FlyReflectCeil and ftCo_MS_FlyReflectWall.
Surface enum/value names use ftCo_Surface_* such as ftCo_Surface_LeftWall and ftCo_Surface_Ceiling.
Anonymous small-data constants in symbols.txt use @NNN labels when no semantic name is established, replacing ftCo_804D8C40-style address-derived names in this PR.
The added inline duplicate was named ftCo_800C1718_inline, making the codegen purpose explicit without claiming a new original symbol.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use #pragma dont_inline on a helper when the original emitted a callable function, and use a separate inline clone where another original callsite inlined the same logic.
Use PAD_STACK(0x10) for stack reservation when normal dummy arrays risk changing rodata or initialization behavior.
Use scalar pad locals such as u32 pad1 = 0 instead of initialized u8 arrays when tuning stack layout and avoiding unwanted constant data.
Use explicit temporaries ret and tmp with comparisons against false/0 to shape branch structure in boolean-returning collision functions.
Use gobj->user_data instead of GET_FIGHTER(gobj) in a specific function when macro expansion/register behavior does not match.
Replace sqrtf with an inline fake_sqrtf using __frsqrte, three Newton refinement steps, double constants .5 and 3.0, and volatile float y to match PowerPC floating-point codegen.
Adjust declaration order and delayed assignment of pointers such as Fighter* fp to affect register allocation and stack frame layout.
review_feedback: The PR author stated this was their first match PR and specifically asked about stack padding at the beginning of a function without creating rodata.
No human review comments were present in the provided slice.

The decomp.dev bot reported GALE01 improvements: matched code 59.14% (+0.03%, +1268 bytes), linked code 33.30% (+0.05%, +1860 bytes), matched data 37.69% (+32 bytes), and linked data 32.19% (+32 bytes).
The bot listed 4 new matches for main/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_FlyReflect: .sdata2, ftCo_800C17CC, ftCo_800C18A8, and ftCo_FlyReflect_Coll.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename or document fake_sqrtf or move it to a shared helper.
Search for other ftCommon match PRs using #pragma dont_inline plus an inline duplicate helper.
Search for PRs discussing PAD_STACK versus initialized dummy arrays causing rodata.
Check whether anonymous @221/@259/@260/@261/@262 .sdata2 labels receive semantic names in future cleanup PRs.
